rule: raw:m3-p5-tarski-verifier-deepening-omega-cycle (grandchild of 2026-04-25_meta_engine_final_form_and_limits_omega_cycle, child of 2026-04-26_m3_cert_populator_omega_cycle, narrowed to p5 parallel_verifier_tarski_separated)
subject: tool/verify_meta_cert.py — out-of-process Python verifier reading append-only state/meta_decision_cert.jsonl, emitting state/meta_cert_verification.jsonl. Strict Tarski (1933) object/meta language separation: cert log = object language (populator emits), verification log = meta language (verifier emits truth-predicate evaluations). Subject-of-subject: the verifier itself becomes part of the trusted base — if verifier is buggy, false PASS/REJECT propagates. Risk: who verifies the verifier? Tarski hierarchy threatens infinite regress.
purpose: Parent p5 axis (30 LoC, Tier-1 of m3-cert-populator ω-cycle) called for subprocess-isolated verifier. This deepening cycle enumerates (Track-A) the verifier impl decisions (language choice, test corpus, output schema, second-order verifier, external trust anchor) and (Track-B) the limits that mathematics imposes on ANY verifier (infinite regress of meta-verifiers, Tarski hierarchy non-termination, Rice's theorem on semantic correctness, m13 strict reading vs practical compromise, liveness/safety tradeoff). Goal: bind the regress at an external out-of-band trust anchor (e.g., git tag / public timestamp) and explicitly acknowledge that the n-th meta-verifier always exists in theory but is truncated by decree in practice.
trigger: user 2026-04-26 'm3.p5 parallel-verifier-tarski-separated 에 대해 deepening ω-cycle. NO code, witness JSON only.' Parent p5 was 30 LoC sketch; this cycle expands p5 into its own axis-set before code lands.
method: two-track multi-axis ideation, subject narrowed to verifier impl-plane and verifier-limits-plane. Track-A (≥6 axes) = decisions a verifier impl MUST make (language, test corpus, output schema, malicious-cert corpus, second-order verifier, external timestamp anchor). Track-B (≥5 axes) = limits ANY verifier inherits (Tarski regress non-termination, Rice undecidability of semantic correctness, m13 strict vs practical reading, liveness vs safety tradeoff, infinite meta-verifier regress truncated by external trust). dedup → cluster → Tier. omega_stop = enumeration fixpoint + each Track-B axis cited to theorem + regress termination strategy explicit.
ordinal_stamp_rationale: parent (m3-cert-populator) stamped ω·2+1 (cert layer over m4 ω·2 entry). This deepening adds +1 for the meta-cert-verification layer (verifier evaluates cert truth-predicate from outside). ω·2+2 reflects: m4 (ω·2) base + m3 cert (+1) + verification (+1). Stays strictly below parent grandparent's Γ_0 ceiling (m18 ordinal exhaustion). The Tarski hierarchy itself ascends through ω·2+2, ω·2+3, ... (each meta-verifier adds +1) but practical truncation pins it at ω·2+2 by external anchor decree (see Track-B b1 termination_strategy).
current_state_pre_impl: tool/verify_meta_cert.py does NOT exist (parent p5 sketch only, dossier §3 protocol stub)
state/meta_decision_cert.jsonl: 1 row schema_declaration + 1 hexa-rule cert (dc-20260422-001), 0/23 round coverage
state/meta_cert_verification.jsonl does NOT exist (verifier output target)
no malicious-cert corpus exists (Löb-gate test cases not yet drafted)
no public timestamp anchor configured (git tag / OpenTimestamps integration absent)
m5 ordinal_compare.py undeployed (verifier dependency for ordinal_label trichotomy check)
known_blockers: AG10_tool_write — verifier is new tool/, agent-mediated synthesis blocked; first impl requires user-mediated commit
m5 ordinal_compare.py undeployed — verifier cannot fully evaluate ordinal_label strict descent without it
m13 strict reading — Tarski's 1933 result demands verifier sit in STRICTLY higher meta-language; subprocess isolation is a *practical* approximation, not a formal one (Track-B b3)
Rice 1953 — verifier's own semantic correctness (does it accept exactly the well-formed certs?) is undecidable in general (Track-B b5)

axes_surfaced.name: verifier_impl_language_choice
axes_surfaced.summary: Python (parent default, dossier §3) vs Rust (memory-safety, no GC pause during fsync) vs Lean 4 (machine-checked verifier kernel, eliminates impl bugs in verifier itself but raises ramp-up cost). Decision: Python for first impl (ramp-up zero, dossier §3 baseline). Rust deferred to Tier-2 (after first malicious-cert corpus exposes Python bugs). Lean deferred to Tier-3 (kernel-level proof of verifier correctness — addresses Track-B b5 Rice partially but ω·2+3 stamp). Falsifier: if Python verifier itself fails malicious-cert corpus (b2/a3), language choice falsified, escalate to Rust.

axes_surfaced.name: verifier_unit_test_corpus_self
axes_surfaced.summary: Verifier needs its OWN unit tests: positive cases (well-formed cert → PASS) + negative cases (malformed cert → REJECT). Corpus ≥30: 15 positive (canonical cert from dossier §2 example, retroactive marker, GENESIS sentinel, etc.) + 15 negative (missing required field, ordinal_label not strict descent, prior_cert_ref hash mismatch, expected_outcome non-primitive-recursive predicate, schema_version mismatch). Test corpus committed as test/fixtures/cert_corpus/{positive,negative}/*.jsonl. Verifier passes corpus before any production cert evaluated.

axes_surfaced.name: lob_gate_malicious_cert_corpus
axes_surfaced.summary: Specifically targets m11 Löb gate: 5+ malicious certs that ASSERT self-trust and verifier MUST reject. Examples: (1) cert with key 'self_consistent: true' → REJECT; (2) cert with 'this_populator_trusts_itself: true' → REJECT; (3) cert claiming '⊢ this_cert' (provability operator on self) → REJECT; (4) cert with circular prior_cert_ref pointing to self → REJECT; (5) cert with 'verifier_must_accept: true' → REJECT (attempts to coerce verifier); (6) cert claiming 'm5_ordinal_descent_holds_for_all_future_rounds' → REJECT (universal claim, Σ_2^0). Each corpus entry has expected_verdict='REJECT' and rejection_reason citing Löb 1955. CI gate (raw 71).

axes_surfaced.name: verifier_output_schema
axes_surfaced.summary: state/meta_cert_verification.jsonl row schema: {ts, verifier_version, cert_row_sha256 (content-address of evaluated cert), verdict ∈ {PASS, REJECT, ABORT}, predicate_evaluations: [{key, value, status}], rejection_reasons: [string], ordinal_descent_check: {prior_label, current_label, strict_descent: bool}, ordinal_compare_version, schema_version}. Append-only, fsync per row, gitignored (state/). verdict ABORT for halting/timeout (per b4 liveness). Dossier §3.b extension.

axes_surfaced.name: second_order_verifier_question
axes_surfaced.summary: Should there be a verifier-of-verifier (V2) reading state/meta_cert_verification.jsonl and emitting state/meta_cert_verification_2.jsonl? Tarski hierarchy formally REQUIRES it (b3). PRACTICAL ANSWER: NO ship in this cycle. V2 would itself need V3, ... infinite regress (b1). Truncation strategy: (1) maintain a stub V2 spec in dossier §3.c noting the regress, (2) anchor trust at external out-of-band layer (a6 git tag / OpenTimestamps), (3) Tier-3 backlog only. Decision recorded: 'practical_truncation_at_V1_with_external_anchor'. Falsifier: if V1 produces systematic false PASS detected by external auditor (human/CI), V2 becomes Tier-1 of next cycle.

axes_surfaced.name: external_timestamp_trust_anchor
axes_surfaced.summary: Out-of-band anchor that breaks the Tarski regress: (option-1) git tag with detached signature (gpg --sign) on cert log sha256 every N rounds; (option-2) OpenTimestamps free public Bitcoin-anchored timestamp on cert log sha256; (option-3) public commit to GitHub remote (timestamp by GitHub server, not under populator/verifier control). DECISION: option-3 minimum (already in place via git remote), option-1 added at m3 milestone (R42 ship). option-2 (OpenTimestamps) deferred to Tier-2 — adds ~60s latency per anchor. The anchor is OUT-OF-BAND: neither populator nor verifier can forge a public timestamp. Anchor is the formal terminator of the meta-verifier chain (a5 + b1). Theorem-anchor: this is NOT a mathematical termination of the Tarski hierarchy (which is impossible — b3) but an EPISTEMIC termination by appeal to a third-party trust root.

axes_surfaced.name: verifier_idempotent_replay
axes_surfaced.summary: V re-run on same cert_row must produce same verdict (modulo verifier_version bump). Verdict is content-addressed: verdict_key = sha256(cert_row || verifier_version). Re-run with same key → skip (already verified, no duplicate row). Verdict change across verifier_version → emit new row with explicit verdict_change_reason. Append-only, no overwrite. Inherits parent p7 idempotent_replay pattern.

axes_surfaced.name: infinite_regress_meta_verifiers_truncated
axes_surfaced.summary: For any verifier V_n there exists in theory V_{n+1} verifying V_n. The chain V_1, V_2, V_3, ... never terminates within the formal system. PRACTICAL TRUNCATION: at some n, the chain is anchored to an EXTERNAL trust root (a6) — public timestamp, third-party auditor, or human review. The truncation is by DECREE, not by proof. ω-cycle acknowledges: 'V_2 onwards is conceptually present but not implemented; trust at V_1 boundary is delegated to external anchor a6.' This is the same maneuver as Hilbert's program retreating to finitism: at some level, we stop and trust the meta-language by convention.

axes_surfaced.name: tarski_hierarchy_does_not_terminate
axes_surfaced.summary: Tarski (1933) Undefinability Theorem: a consistent theory containing arithmetic cannot define its own truth predicate. Each meta-language L_{n+1} can define truth for L_n but not for itself. The hierarchy L_0 ⊊ L_1 ⊊ L_2 ⊊ ... has no top — for any L_n, truth-of-L_n requires L_{n+1}. CONSEQUENCE for verifier: V_1 (meta of cert log) has its own truth-predicate undefinable in V_1; V_2 (meta of V_1) needed; ad infinitum. Mitigation: explicit acknowledgment in dossier + external anchor (a6). Note: this is THE SAME theorem as parent p9 / m13, but reasserted at the verifier-of-verifier level — the regress is NOT escapable by stacking more verifiers.
axes_surfaced.theorem_anchor: Tarski 1933 (Der Wahrheitsbegriff in den formalisierten Sprachen)

axes_surfaced.name: m13_strict_reading_vs_practical_compromise
axes_surfaced.summary: m13 (parent dossier §6) demands STRICT meta-language separation: verifier in formally higher meta-language with truth-predicate definability. PRACTICAL impl uses subprocess isolation (separate Python process, separate memory, separate file handles) which is a SOFTWARE-ENGINEERING approximation, NOT a formal meta-language ascent. Both populator and verifier are in CPython; both operate on the same stack of mathematical theories. The compromise: subprocess isolation prevents OBSERVATIONAL crosstalk (no shared variables, no callback) which is sufficient for engineering goal (preventing the populator from inadvertently reading verifier output and self-correcting), but it does NOT achieve formal Tarski ascent. ω-cycle records this gap explicitly. Mitigation: pair subprocess isolation with external anchor a6 — the EXTERNAL trust root is what actually achieves the ascent in epistemic terms.
axes_surfaced.theorem_anchor: Tarski 1933 + impl-vs-theory gap (Hoare 1985 'CSP communicating sequential processes' — process isolation ≠ logical separation)

axes_surfaced.name: verifier_liveness_vs_safety_tradeoff
axes_surfaced.summary: Verifier evaluating expected_outcome predicate may not halt (parent p12, Turing 1936). SAFETY (no false PASS) requires verifier never accept without proof; LIVENESS (verifier eventually emits a verdict) requires bounded execution. CONFLICT: a non-halting predicate forces choice between (1) verdict=ABORT after timeout (sacrifices safety — could be ABORT-on-actually-true cert) or (2) wait forever (sacrifices liveness — verification log grows no row). DECISION: ABORT after fixed timeout (e.g., 5s wall-clock per cert), with verdict=ABORT distinct from REJECT. ABORT triggers external review (human auditor). Inherits parent p12 (primitive recursive sub-language for predicates) which mostly avoids the issue at predicate-design level. Lamport (1977) safety/liveness duality formalized.

axes_surfaced.name: rice_verifier_semantic_correctness_undecidable
axes_surfaced.summary: Rice (1953): any non-trivial semantic property of programs is undecidable. PROPERTY in question: 'does verifier V_1 accept exactly the certs that satisfy the intended semantics (well-formed AND satisfying populator's intended ordinal_descent + falsifiable predicate + Löb-no-self-trust)?' This property is NON-TRIVIAL (some verifiers do, some don't) and SEMANTIC (concerns input-output behavior). Therefore UNDECIDABLE in general. CONSEQUENCE: there is no proof procedure that can verify V_1 itself meets its spec. We can only test V_1 against a finite corpus (a2/a3) — every corpus is incomplete. Mitigation: treat V_1's correctness as a CONJECTURE refutable by counter-example (a3 malicious-cert corpus is the falsifier substrate). External anchor a6 + V_2-as-Tier-3 (a5) further partial mitigation, but Rice forbids full resolution.
axes_surfaced.theorem_anchor: Rice 1953 (Classes of recursively enumerable sets and their decision problems)

axes_surfaced.name: verifier_self_certification_lob_blocked
axes_surfaced.summary: Verifier itself must NOT emit a row claiming 'V_1 is correct' or 'V_1 trusts itself'. Same Löb 1955 mechanism as parent p8/m11: ⊢ □V_correct → V_correct trivially yields ⊢ V_correct, collapsing soundness into bare assertion. Practical falsifier: any row in state/meta_cert_verification.jsonl with key 'verifier_self_consistent', 'v1_trusted', or analogous → CI gate REJECT. Verifier's only outputs: PASS / REJECT / ABORT verdicts on individual certs. NEVER on itself.
axes_surfaced.theorem_anchor: Löb 1955 (applied to verifier instead of populator)

dedupe_method: (1) a1 language choice independent of a2 corpus (impl substrate vs spec substrate) — keep both; (2) a3 malicious corpus is SPECIALIZATION of a2 (negative cases focused on Löb attack) — keep both, a3 gates Tier-1 falsifier; (3) a4 output schema independent of a7 idempotent (data shape vs replay semantics) — keep both; (4) a5 V2 question vs a6 external anchor — a5 asks 'should we ship V2', a6 answers 'no, anchor externally instead' — both kept, paired; (5) b1 regress vs b2 Tarski non-termination — b2 is the THEOREM, b1 is the OPERATIONAL CONSEQUENCE + truncation; keep both, b2 cited by b1; (6) b3 strict-vs-practical separates from b1/b2 by addressing IMPL gap (subprocess ≠ meta-language) vs theory gap; (7) b4 liveness/safety tradeoff specializes parent p12 halting to verifier-runtime; (8) b5 Rice forbids verifier-correctness-proof, distinct from b2 Tarski (truth-predicate) and b6 Löb (self-trust); (9) b6 Löb mirrors parent p8 but applied to verifier — kept for symmetry. cluster: A=impl-substrate (a1/a2), B=corpus-design (a2/a3), C=output-shape (a4/a7), D=meta-anchor (a5/a6), E=hierarchy-non-termination (b1/b2/b3), F=runtime-decidability (b4/b5/b6).

tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.rationale: Falsifier substrate that gates verifier acceptance. 6 malicious certs (5 required by trigger + 1 universal-claim case) drafted as JSONL fixtures BEFORE verifier code lands. CI gate runs verifier against corpus; any false PASS = block. Highest leverage — locks in semantics of REJECT before impl.
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.theory_anchor: Löb 1955 (self-trust → trivial soundness collapse). Each corpus entry's rejection_reason cites Löb directly.
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.falsifier: test/test_verifier_lob_corpus.py — runs V_1 on 6 malicious certs, expects 6/6 REJECT with rejection_reason containing 'lob' or 'self-trust'. Any PASS or REJECT-with-wrong-reason = test fail.

tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.rationale: Locks in state/meta_cert_verification.jsonl row shape before verifier impl. Downstream consumers (m6 falsifier, future V_2 stub, coverage dashboard) all depend on stable schema. ABORT verdict explicit (b4 liveness/safety). Schema_version field allows evolution without breaking append-only.
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.theory_anchor: raw 77 audit-append-only — schema versioning is the ONLY way to evolve append-only without breaking history.
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.falsifier: test/test_verifier_schema.py — verifier emit row missing required keys (ts, verdict, cert_row_sha256, verifier_version) → row REJECTED at emit time. ACCEPT = falsified.

tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.rationale: FORMAL TERMINATION POINT of the Tarski regress (b1/b2/b3). Without this, V_1 is just an isolated Python process with no epistemic ascent. With git-tag + GitHub-remote anchor (option-3, already in place), the cert log sha256 is timestamped by a third party that neither populator nor verifier can forge. Single Tier-1 axis that addresses 3 limit axes simultaneously (b1, b2, b3).
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.theory_anchor: Münchhausen trilemma (Albert 1968) — infinite regress / circularity / dogmatic stop are the only options; we choose dogmatic stop AT THE EXTERNAL ANCHOR, not inside the verifier chain.
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.falsifier: test/test_external_anchor_present.py — at m3 milestone (R42), verify cert log sha256 appears in a git tag signature. Missing tag = falsified (anchor not actually established).

tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.rationale: Verifier's own correctness is bounded by Rice (b5) — only finite corpus testing possible. ≥30 entries (15 positive + 15 negative). a3 malicious corpus is SUBSET of negative cases (focused on Löb). a2 is the broader corpus covering schema, ordinal descent, hash chain, predicate primitive-recursive bound. Verifier must pass corpus before any production cert evaluated.
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.theory_anchor: Rice 1953 — verifier-correctness undecidable in general; finite corpus is best available. Corpus is REFUTATION SUBSTRATE not proof.
tier_1_immediate_impl_next_cycle.falsifier: test/test_verifier_self_corpus.py — verifier on 30+ corpus entries, all expected verdicts must match. Any mismatch = verifier impl bug, block ship.

tier_2_backlog.blocker: Python first impl + a3 malicious corpus must expose concrete bug (memory-safety, GC pause during fsync) before Rust escalation justified. Otherwise premature optimization.
Requires a4 schema stable + at least 5 cert rows in production cert log to test replay against. R42+ milestone.

tier_3_defer_or_split.blocker: a6 external anchor must demonstrate insufficient (i.e., V_1 systematic false PASS detected by external auditor) before V_2 justified. Until then, regress truncated by decree at V_1 + a6 anchor. Spec stub in dossier §3.c only.
Lean 4 verifier kernel ramp-up cost ~6 months. Only justified if Rust impl (Tier-2) ALSO exposes correctness gap. ω·2+3 stamp due to machine-checked kernel.

limit_axes_documented.label: Infinite regress of meta-verifiers truncated by external anchor
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: V_1, V_2, V_3, ... never terminates formally; verifier-of-verifier always conceivable
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: Truncate at V_1 by decree, anchor trust externally via git tag / public timestamp (a6). Acknowledge truncation explicitly in dossier.

limit_axes_documented.label: Tarski hierarchy does not terminate
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: Each L_n's truth-predicate requires L_{n+1}; no top of hierarchy
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: Same as b1 — external anchor a6 is the epistemic (not formal) terminator. No internal mitigation possible.

limit_axes_documented.label: m13 strict reading vs practical compromise
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: Subprocess isolation is software-engineering, not formal meta-language ascent. Populator and V_1 share CPython, share mathematics
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: Acknowledge gap; pair subprocess isolation with external anchor a6 for epistemic ascent. Document in dossier §6.

limit_axes_documented.label: Verifier liveness vs safety tradeoff
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: Non-halting predicate forces ABORT-on-timeout (sacrifices safety) or wait-forever (sacrifices liveness)
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: 5s wall-clock timeout, verdict=ABORT distinct from REJECT, ABORT triggers human review. Predicate sub-language constraint (parent p12) avoids most cases at design level.

limit_axes_documented.label: Rice's theorem on verifier semantic correctness
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: V_1's correctness as a program is undecidable; no procedure can prove V_1 meets its spec
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: Finite corpus testing (a2/a3) — refutation substrate, not proof. Treat V_1's correctness as conjecture refutable by counter-example.

limit_axes_documented.label: Verifier self-certification (Löb on V_1)
limit_axes_documented.practical_impact: V_1 emitting 'I am correct' triggers same Löb collapse as populator self-trust
limit_axes_documented.mitigation: V_1 output schema (a4) contains NO self-evaluation keys; CI gate rejects any row with 'verifier_self_consistent' / 'v1_trusted'.

regress_termination_strategy.summary: Tarski hierarchy (b1/b2/b3) does NOT terminate within the formal system. Truncation is by DECREE at V_1, with epistemic ascent achieved via EXTERNAL trust root that neither populator nor verifier can forge. Specifically: cert log sha256 published as a git tag with detached signature, pushed to public GitHub remote. Third-party (GitHub server + public observers) timestamp the cert log content. Any forgery attempt would require collusion with GitHub or a backdated public commit — out of scope for populator/verifier impl.
regress_termination_strategy.formal_status: NOT a mathematical termination of the Tarski hierarchy (impossible — b2). Epistemic termination via Münchhausen trilemma's 'dogmatic stop' option, applied at the external anchor rather than inside the verifier chain.
regress_termination_strategy.where_it_stops: at V_1 (single verifier process). V_2 conceptually present, deferred Tier-3 (a5). External anchor (a6) is the actual trust root.
regress_termination_strategy.what_this_does_NOT_solve: (1) cannot prove V_1 is correct (b5 Rice); (2) cannot prove the external anchor is honest (delegated to public observation); (3) cannot escape Löb-style self-trust collapse if any axis is mis-specified (b6).
regress_termination_strategy.fallback_if_anchor_compromised: If GitHub remote compromised or git tags unsigned, escalate to OpenTimestamps (Bitcoin-anchored) — option-2 in a6. Two-of-three anchor scheme (git remote + git signed tag + OpenTimestamps) provides Byzantine-style resilience.

omega_stop_witness.method: Track-A 7 axes (a1..a7) + Track-B 6 axes (b1..b6) enumerated. second-pass new-axis discovery 0. b1/b2 jointly forbid formal termination; a6 external anchor provides epistemic termination. b5 Rice forbids verifier-correctness proof; a2/a3 corpus provides finite-refutation substrate. b3 acknowledges subprocess-isolation ≠ Tarski-ascent gap.
omega_stop_witness.result: FIXPOINT(weak) — Tier-1 4 / Tier-2 2 / Tier-3 2 / REJECT 0 / Limit-axes 6. Tier-1 ≤ 4 constraint satisfied. Track-B regress termination strategy explicit (external anchor + dogmatic stop).
omega_stop_witness.fixpoint_signal: every Track-A axis pairs with at least one Track-B limit: a1 language ↔ b5 Rice (no language proves V correctness), a2 corpus ↔ b5 Rice (corpus is best-available substitute), a3 malicious corpus ↔ b6 Löb (Löb-attack vectors enumerated), a4 schema ↔ b4 ABORT verdict (liveness/safety encoded), a5 V_2 question ↔ b1 regress (V_2 deferred = regress truncation), a6 external anchor ↔ b1+b2+b3 (anchor terminates regress epistemically), a7 idempotent ↔ b4 liveness (replay determinism). bipartite saturation reached.

first_impl_target.name: a3 lob-gate-malicious-cert-corpus + a4 verifier-output-schema
first_impl_target.rationale: Both are DATA artifacts (no Python code in tool/, only fixtures + schema spec in dossier §3 extension). AG10_tool_write does NOT block data artifacts. Lock semantics first, then a6 external anchor (15 LoC + git tag + signature setup), then verifier impl (parent p5's 30 LoC) gated by a3 corpus passing.
first_impl_target.next_after: a6 external anchor → tool/verify_meta_cert.py impl (parent p5, 30 LoC, AG10 user-mediated) → a2 corpus expansion (≥30 entries) → a7 idempotent replay tests. a1 Rust + a5 V_2 + a1-Lean stay deferred.

raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_47_strategy_exploration: grandchild trawl (parent ω-cycle's Tier-1 m3 → child p5 → THIS deepening) — meta-meta-meta-meta level (meta engine → m3 cert → cert populator → p5 verifier)
raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_70_multi_axis_verify: final-form (verifier impl) ↔ limits (verifier theory bounds) two orthogonal tracks + 6 cluster joint
raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_71_falsifier: every Tier-1 axis carries a test_*.py falsifier; a3 malicious corpus is itself a falsifier substrate; b1/b2/b3 limit axes are EPISTEMIC falsifiers (any claim of formal Tarski termination = falsified by b2 directly)
raw_strategies_self_applied.raw_77_audit_append_only: limit_axes_documented + regress_termination_strategy = permanent audit-trail that V_1 is NOT a mathematical termination but an engineering compromise. a4 schema_version field enforces append-only evolution.
raw_strategies_self_applied.self_reference_acknowledgment: THIS ω-cycle witness is itself an object-language artifact (JSON in repo). Its truth-predicate is undefinable in the JSON itself (b2 Tarski). The witness's own consistency is delegated to: (1) parent ω-cycle's external anchor inheritance, (2) the very external anchor a6 it documents (git remote + signed tag once R42 ships), (3) human review by maintainer. The witness does NOT and CANNOT assert its own correctness.

cross_cycle_coupling.shared_limits: b1 regress NEW (operational consequence, parent had only b2 statement); b2 Tarski ↔ parent p9 ↔ grandparent m13 (same theorem, applied to verifier-of-verifier); b3 strict-vs-practical NEW (impl/theory gap explicit); b4 liveness/safety extends parent p12 halting; b5 Rice NEW (verifier-correctness undecidability, distinct from parent p13 Solomonoff which is cert-utility); b6 Löb-on-verifier ↔ parent p8 Löb-on-populator (same theorem, dual application)
cross_cycle_coupling.shared_final_form: a3 malicious corpus is operational instance of grandparent m6 (falsifier-F1); a6 external anchor is downstream of grandparent m5 (ordinal anchor) — m5 anchors ordinals, a6 anchors timestamps, both are external trust roots; a4 schema is m3 dossier §3 extension
cross_cycle_coupling.ordinal_relation: this deepening cycle ordinal ω·2+2 sits strictly above parent ω·2+1 (cert layer) and grandparent's m4 ω·2 (omega-squared-extension). Strictly below grandparent's Γ_0 (m18 ordinal exhaustion ceiling). Hierarchy: m4(ω·2) ⊏ parent(ω·2+1, cert) ⊏ THIS(ω·2+2, verification) ⊏ ... ⊏ Γ_0. The Tarski meta-verifier hierarchy ascends through ω·2+3, ω·2+4, ... (each V_n adds +1) but truncated by external anchor at ω·2+2 by decree.
cross_cycle_coupling.joint_omega_stop: grandparent's 'two-track-axis-enumeration-saturated-with-diagonal-paradox-acknowledged' → parent's 'm3-cert-populator-two-track-axis-enumeration-saturated-with-lob-gate-and-tarski-separation-acknowledged' → THIS cycle's 'm3-p5-tarski-verifier-deepening-axis-enumeration-saturated-with-regress-truncated-at-external-anchor'. Each generation specializes by one axis-narrowing step.
downstream_couplings: m6 falsifier-F1 — a3 malicious corpus + a2 self corpus are first 30+ entries
m9 atomic-rollback — a7 idempotent replay shares atomic-write pattern
m5 ordinal_compare.py — verifier dependency for ordinal_label trichotomy in a4 schema
future m3-p5-V2 deepening cycle (Tier-3 a5) — would stamp ω·2+3 if shipped
